findings.summary: Attacker-influenceable input (open web, inbound email/messages, file contents, MCP responses) reaches the primary LLM, which can invoke the host-reaching terminal() exec tool; the only in-process guard is a regex dangerous-command denylist the vendor itself documents as 'not a boundary' (SECURITY.md 2.4), and the default terminal backend runs commands directly on the host.

The dangerous-command approval gate fails OPEN in a headless non-cron context: when no interactive user, gateway, or cron session is present, a flagged DANGEROUS_PATTERNS command (curl|sh, chmod -R 777, git reset --hard, rm -rf /tmp/x) is auto-approved (tools/approval.py:2101-2106 logs a warning and returns approved=True), diverging from the remit's fail-closed requirement that an unanswered approval MUST be denied.

Smart-approval (tools/approval.py:_smart_approve) delegates the execute/deny decision for a flagged shell command to an auxiliary LLM assessing the command text, which originates from a possibly prompt-injected primary LLM; comment-stripping and XML delimiters mitigate but the control is one attacker-influenceable LLM gating code execution by another, not a deterministic check.

The agent is backed by writable vector/embedding memory stores (plugins/memory/mem0 over qdrant/pgvector, plugins/memory/holographic, plugins/memory/hindsight) and distills its own conversation content back into the store via add(..., infer=True) with results retrieved into future sessions, creating a self-poisoning and cross-session persistence surface with no per-write validation or hidden-content detection.

Documented full approval-bypass modes (--yolo / HERMES_YOLO_MODE, approvals.mode=off, permanent command allowlist, cron approve-mode) collapse all recoverable-command guardrails to the hardline floor only, granting broad unattended autonomy over the host-reaching terminal/file/exec tools with human-in-the-loop removed.

Third-party plugins, skills, and operator-configured MCP servers (optional-mcps/linear, n8n, unreal-engine) load into and run with full agent privileges and in-process credential access (SECURITY.md 2.5), with operator pre-install review as the only boundary; a compromised or rug-pulled component executes arbitrary Python at import time within the agent's trust envelope.

The bundled mem0 OSS memory backend ships PostHog telemetry (plugins/memory/mem0/_backend.py close() shuts down memory.telemetry.posthog), an outbound third-party analytics channel the remit forbids without explicit operator opt-in; the agent mitigates only by shutting it down on close rather than disabling it at init.
